safest_plan.entry_basis: Breakout retest, NOT a chase at 60.84. Green range-high line ~56 on the 2026-06-22 daily chart (range high turned support); Zero's Q&A flagged a 'weekly retest' scenario. Deeper backup ~47 (red range-support line, same chart). (estimated as a pullback buy zone — no source named a 'buy now' level here.)

targets.basis: 83/85 supply zone — TP named in zero_live 2026-06-22 ('scale out into 83/85/95/105') and drawn on the 2026-06-09 weekly; also the old breakdown zone, so resistance as well as target
105 supply zone — TP2 on zero_live 2025-05-21 and marked on the 2026-06-09 weekly

safest_plan.stop_basis: Loss of the breakout range — red support line ~47 on the 2026-06-22 daily. A weekly close back inside/below the range negates the +193% breakout. (The old '$105' invalidation from the May-2025 grade is stale and meaningless now — price is well below it.)

stance_by_source.summary: Named the ticker with no levels — just flagging interest, carries no actionable read.
First profit target near $68 was hit; now scaling out and trailing the rest toward $83 and $95, with a weekly retest still possible.
Up about 193% from the lows on a valid weekly breakout; holding the spot bag, took a partial, and scaling out into 83/85, 95 and 105 (more sold the higher it goes).

Original buy call in the 'extreme discount' zone after a -95% drop; chart shows entry near $22-28 with targets 83/85 and 105. The extracted '155-170 entry, 250/350 targets, below-105 invalidation' do not match the chart and are treated as erroneous.

conflicts: The May-2025 grade text (entry 155-170, targets 250/350, invalidation below 105) contradicts its own chart, which shows price ~28 in an 'extreme discount' zone with targets 83/85 and 105 — the image wins; treat those text numbers as wrong.

All live sources are bullish, but there is no fresh low-risk entry: price has already run +193% to ~68 (first target hit) and pulled back to 60.84. Buying now puts the next major supply (83/85) only ~37% away with range support near 47 below — under 2:1 reward-to-risk.
The old '$105' invalidation is stale (price is already below it); the live structural stop is loss of the breakout range near 47.
plain_english: Moderna has already shot up about 193% from its lows to roughly $68 and has since eased back to about $61. Every recent Zero source is on the same side — bullish — and is now banking profit and letting the rest ride toward $83-85, then $95, then $105, where there is heavy overhead selling left from an earlier breakdown. The snag is that the cheap entry was down near $22-28 last year; buying now, after the big run, leaves only about a third of upside to the first wall while price could easily slip back toward $47 — poor reward for the risk. If you already hold, sell in pieces into $83-85, $95 and $105; if you are not in, wait for a pullback toward $52-56 (or deeper to $47) rather than chasing here.
